title: Sison aureum

side_effects:
Sison aureum, also known as Mexican golden parsley, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, but its use can have some side effects. 

The consumption of Sison aureum may cause allergic reactions in some individuals, such as skin irritation, itching, and hives. 

Its seeds may also interact with certain medications and exacerbate conditions like hypertension and respiratory issues. 

Furthermore, excessive consumption of Sison aureum may lead to stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in sensitive individuals. 

More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Sison aureum, and its use should be approached with caution under the guidance of a medical professional.
intro: Sison aureum, also known as Golden Alexanders, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow or golden flowers and fern-like foliage. The health benefits of Sison aureum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sison aureum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory and immune system health.
